_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that capital letters are used in titles and commas are used to separate items in a list.

_source.w: We are learning that capital letters are used in titles and commas are used to separate items in a list.
y: Using capitals and commas correctly makes our writing professional and easy to read.
Punctuation rules are shared conventions that everyone follows, so learning them helps us write like real authors.
These are practical skills we use every time we write.
eg: We look at book titles and notice which words have capital letters.
We write a list of items using commas to separate them — like 'apples, bananas, oranges and grapes'.
We check our own titles to see if we used capital letters correctly.
We add commas to a list in our writing and read it aloud to hear the natural pauses.
We find commas in a text and discuss what they are separating.
We edit a piece of writing that is missing commas in lists and capitals in titles.
